theoremName: adjoint Green boundary diagnostic consequence theorem

diagnosticAvailableStatus.label: sampled adjoint-boundary diagnostic is available
diagnosticAvailableStatus.reason: The finite-difference diagnostic reports active-range, IBP-defect, and eta-size summaries.

finiteActiveRangeDiagnosticStatus.label: finite active range diagnostic
finiteActiveRangeDiagnosticStatus.reason: The diagnostic confirms the finite active block range identity.

sampledIbpFailureModeStatus.label: sampled pseudoinverse kernel is not the continuum Green solution
sampledIbpFailureModeStatus.reason: The sampled-kernel IBP defect is intentionally retained as a diagnostic failure mode.  It shows that differentiating the coarse pseudoinverse kernel does not solve the adjoint Green BVP.

continuumAdjointGreenProofStatus.label: continuum adjoint Green proof not claimed here
continuumAdjointGreenProofStatus.reason: This consequence does not close the continuum adjoint Green problem; endpoint range and active trace-range theorems remain the proof route.

interpretation: Finite-difference P^*K_N and endpoint concomitant diagnostic. Large IBP defects indicate the need for an analytic adjoint Green construction rather than differentiating coarse sampled kernel coefficients.
proof: Import the finite-difference adjoint Green boundary diagnostic.
Expose only the summary fields consumed by continuum_trace_to_source_green_kernel.
Do not promote sampled IBP evidence to a continuum adjoint Green theorem.
notAProofDependency: The continuum adjoint Green BVP is handled by the endpoint range and active trace-range theorem chain.  This record is retained only as a sampled-kernel diagnostic.
